summary: Draft playbook rules were generated from repository inventory, practice candidates, clarification questions, and recorded answers. High-confidence practices were accepted directly, and medium-confidence practices were accepted only where the recorded answers resolved their clarification needs.

rules.title: Use npm scripts as the supported project entrypoints
rules.rule: Run project build, type-check, development, packaging, and start workflows through the npm scripts declared in package.json.

rules.title: Use the declared check script for TypeScript validation
rules.rule: Before delivering changes that affect TypeScript source or tests, run the declared npm check script unless the task context makes that impossible.

rules.title: Place automated tests under the tests directory
rules.rule: Add or update automated tests under the top-level tests directory, following the existing nested areas when they fit the changed behavior.

rules.title: Keep executor runtime defaults in src/executors/configs
rules.rule: Add new executor runtime configuration modules under src/executors/configs when introducing comparable executor defaults.

rules.title: Keep environment examples limited to required variables
rules.rule: When required environment variables or runtime environment expectations change, update .env.example for those requirements. Treat .agentweaver/.env as a local runtime file that is excluded from committed practice guidance except as evidence that local configuration exists.

rules.title: Keep repository maintenance scripts under scripts
rules.rule: Place repository maintenance automation under the top-level scripts directory when adding automation comparable to the existing maintenance script. No repository-wide .mjs module-format or naming-style requirement is established by the recorded answers.
